description: Bundled drug-interaction landscape for sub-AUD dose disulfiram in a gout-management context. Categorized by (1) DER-stacking, (2) CYP-mediated PK, (3) gout-specific co-medications, (4) ethanol-stacking from incidental sources.
source_primary: wiki/disulfiram.md §Expanded Contraindications and Drug Interactions (synthesized from supplements-stack.md 2026-04-26)

DER_stacking_agents.mechanism: Independent ALDH inhibitor; additive DER risk
MAO interaction + hepatic stress; ALDH effect minor

CYP_PK_interactions.advice: INR monitoring; potentially safer at sub-AUD doses but still requires monitoring

CYP_PK_interactions.DSF_effect: competing hepatic stress; CYP2E1 inhibition by DSF actually protects against APAP-mediated hepatotox in animal models
CYP_PK_interactions.advice: low-dose APAP probably acceptable; avoid high-dose chronic APAP

gout_pharmacology_co_administration.evidence: Asiri 2025 PMC12114764 — ALP 10 mg/kg + DSF 50 mg/kg PO daily × 9d superior to either monotherapy across paw swelling, IL-1β, TNF-α, IL-6, serum urate, renal markers in PO+MSU rat gout model
gout_pharmacology_co_administration.advice: highest-priority companion in a 503A protocol — fills the urate-axis arm that GSDMD-only blockade leaves untouched

gout_pharmacology_co_administration.evidence: wiki/colchicine.md — clinical-grade CP6 prophylaxis at 0.6 mg/d; no DI reports with DSF
DDC metabolites partially renally excreted; probenecid is OAT-substrate inhibitor
gout_pharmacology_co_administration.advice: no specific guidance; standard monitoring sufficient
acceptable for acute flare; avoid chronic stacking

summary_for_503A_protocol: At sub-AUD doses (62.5-125 mg/d), the high-severity DI items remain absolute contraindications (metronidazole, MAOIs). Mid-severity items become manageable with monitoring rather than avoidance. The gout-pharmacology co-administration profile is favorable — allopurinol synergy is documented in a rat gout model, colchicine and low-dose corticosteroids stack without major interaction. Incidental ethanol stacking risk is reduced but not eliminated.
